evidence: Vrijen advocated for using the "dumbest possible AI" (cheaper models like GPT-4o-mini equivalent) and clever scripting/design patterns to simulate complex reasoning.
Careerspan avoids finicky RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) in favor of lightweight, constrained deployment to maintain consistency and lower COGS.
Vrijen explained that LLMs are "deceptively bad" at many tasks without deep context and specialized instruction to prevent generic word substitution.
Vrijen showcased his latest workflow where a single command processes meeting transcripts to generate action items, deliverables, and summaries using GPT-5 Mini.
He implemented a "resume" rule to handle AI timeouts, ensuring the agent picks up where it left off.
Vrijen noted a fascinating interaction between "Howie" and "Zoe" where the two AI agents negotiated rules for coordinating his schedule.
Logan shared insights from a lecture at Harvard about the 10X founder and "automatable payroll." Enterprises are targeting labor budgets to replace human roles with agents.

Vrijen characterized the current AI wave as a new "Industrial Revolution" and argued that LLMs currently favor those who can "reason linguistically" and wield the tools effectively, rather than being truly democratizing.
V shared that he is feeling burnt out and low energy, leading him to take 8-9 days off following this meeting.
